title: From Evolution to Therapy: Precision Targeting of Functionally Divergent Integrins
authors_full: Shixi Sun¹, Yufeng Chen², Li Liu¹,³
authors_short: Shixi Sun Yufeng Chen Li Liu

summary.hook: This poster proposes an evolutionary framework to improve selectivity in integrin-targeted therapies by distinguishing highly conserved RGD-binding cores from lineage-derived...
summary.summary_30s: This poster proposes an evolutionary framework to improve selectivity in integrin-targeted therapies by distinguishing highly conserved RGD-binding cores from lineage-derived, paralog-specific regions within the PS2 α-integrin group (αIIb, α5, αV, α8).

talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Evolutionary comparative analysis \+ structural mapping (computational/phylogenetic framework).. 27 vertebrate species; 4 paralogs; 2 outgroups (listed in Methods box)
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: PS2 integrins originated before vertebrate evolution; four paralogs diverged through stepwise duplication during early vertebrate evolution.

key_numbers: 27 vertebrate species; 4 paralogs; 2 outgroups (listed in Methods box).
\~450 million years of vertebrate evolutionary history (described in introduction). Divergence times displayed in Figure...
Orthologs of PS2 α-integrins (ITGA subunits αIIb, α5, αV, α8) from 27 representative vertebrate species.
Using orthologs from 27 vertebrate species spanning about 450 million years, the authors reconstruct a time-calibrated...
takeaways: PS2 integrins originated before vertebrate evolution; four paralogs diverged through stepwise duplication during early vertebrate evolution.
The MRCA of PS2 integrins likely functioned as a broadly expressed RGD-binding ECM receptor; conserved regions may explain compensatory signaling among paralogs.
The platelet-restricted fibrinogen-binding function of αIIb is a derived innovation linked to vertebrate hemostasis.
